mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for INDPRO month-over-month changes remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and corporate borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: High levels of retail sales combined with high levels of housing starts signal broad-based demand strength that outperforms single-sector indicators, producing positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns through a multiplicative interaction effect.
failure_condition: An F-test for the interaction term RSAFS*HOUST shows no incremental explanatory power (p > 0.05) over the sum of main effects in predicting 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment during late-cycle expansion signals impending demand contraction and corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points over 3 months during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for PAYEMS month-over-month changes remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1000 thousand units signal collapse in residential investment and construction employment, producing negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when HOUST is below 1000.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated federal funds rates and rising unemployment signal a credit-crunch scenario where discount rates spike while earnings fall, producing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns through a multiplicative interaction effect.
failure_condition: The joint effect of FEDFUNDS and UNRATE is not significant (F-test p > 0.05) in predicting 20-day returns beyond individual main effects.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales signals robust consumer spending that drives corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns following positive RSAFS momentum are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).
